meaning: n.发明,发明才能,想出的办法,发明物
examples: The story is told with a complete absence of contrivance.
Martin had learned the contrivance from Joe at the Shelly Hot Springs.
No contrivance can prevent the effect of this distance in weakening government.
Like their very names, Ms Wiggins's characters never quite dispel a whiff of contrivance.
Obama's post-partisan pragmatism, he has confirmed with his inaugural address, was only a useful political contrivance.
